skill.name: klaviyo-automation
skill.description: Automate Klaviyo campaign review tasks through Rube MCP, including campaign discovery, message inspection, tags, pagination, and send job monitoring.
skill.summary: Review and monitor Klaviyo campaigns through Rube MCP workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution, plus weak crypto and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found no executable code, no prompt injection, and no malicious intent in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is legitimate but meaningful networked access to Klaviyo through Rube MCP, which can expose campaign and message data to a third-party integration.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Access to Klaviyo Data
medium_findings.description: The skill requires Rube MCP and instructs users to connect Klaviyo through the Rube endpoint. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it grants a third-party integration access to Klaviyo campaign, message, tag, and send job data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly requires Rube MCP and Klaviyo authentication, then describes retrieving campaign messages and template content. This confirms network and data access risk, while the purpose appears legitimate.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution patterns are Markdown inline code, tool names, parameters, and fenced examples. The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance alerts also match descriptive text or table entries, not executable logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is documentation only and contains no Ruby, shell, or other executable script blocks. The flagged lines are clearly Markdown formatting, API parameter examples, or a quick reference table.

content.value_statement: Klaviyo campaign review can be slow when teams need to inspect status, messages, tags, and send jobs across many campaigns. This skill provides structured Rube MCP workflows for reliable campaign discovery and monitoring.
actual_capabilities: Guides users to search Rube MCP tools before using Klaviyo actions.
Lists and filters Klaviyo campaigns by channel, status, sort order, and pagination cursor.
Retrieves campaign details with optional campaign messages and tags.
Inspects email and SMS campaign message fields, including subject, sender, body, and template data.
Checks campaign tag relationships and send job status with noted rate limits.
Documents common response nesting, pagination, and filter syntax pitfalls.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP server and authenticated Klaviyo connection.
Depends on current Composio tool schemas, so users must search tools before execution.
Focuses on campaign read and monitoring workflows, not full campaign creation.
Can expose marketing campaign content to configured third-party MCP services.

use_cases.title: Audit Draft Campaigns
use_cases.description: Review draft email or SMS campaigns, validate returned statuses, and inspect key message fields before launch.

use_cases.title: Investigate Campaign Content
use_cases.description: Find a campaign, retrieve its message IDs, and inspect subject lines, sender fields, body text, or template links.

use_cases.title: Monitor Send Progress
use_cases.description: Check campaign send job status and handle rate limits while tracking queued, active, completed, or failed sends.

prompt_templates.title: List Campaigns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Klaviyo automation skill to list email campaigns. Search Rube tools first, then return campaign names, IDs, statuses, and pagination notes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Drafts
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all draft Klaviyo email campaigns. Use the proper channel and filter fields, paginate through results, and verify each returned status client-side.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Message Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the message content for this Klaviyo campaign ID. Retrieve the campaign, identify message IDs, and summarize subject, sender, preview text, and body fields.

prompt_templates.title: Build Monitoring Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a repeatable Klaviyo send monitoring workflow. Include tool order, required parameters, pagination handling, rate-limit handling, and status validation steps.

output_examples.input: List draft email campaigns in Klaviyo.
output_examples.output: A campaign review summary with campaign names, IDs, statuses, creation dates, and notes about any mixed status results.

output_examples.input: Inspect the message content for a campaign.
output_examples.output: A concise message report with subject, preview text, sender details, SMS body when present, and template availability.

output_examples.input: Check whether a campaign send job finished.
output_examples.output: A send job status update that states whether the job is queued, in progress, completed, or failed, with rate-limit guidance.

best_practices: Search Rube MCP tools first so parameter names and schemas match the active integration.
Paginate through campaign lists and verify statuses from returned attributes.
Use sparse fieldsets when inspecting messages to reduce unnecessary campaign data exposure.
anti_patterns: Do not assume one campaign list call returns every campaign.
Do not trust server-side status filters without checking returned status fields.
Do not connect Klaviyo through third-party MCP services without confirming account authorization and data handling policies.

faq.question: Does this skill require Klaviyo API keys?
faq.answer: It requires a Rube MCP Klaviyo connection. The setup flow uses Rube connection management rather than direct key entry in the skill.

faq.question: Can it create new campaigns?
faq.answer: The documented workflows focus on listing campaigns, retrieving details, inspecting messages, checking tags, and monitoring send jobs.

faq.question: Why must tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube and Composio tool schemas can change. Searching first helps ensure parameter names and response shapes are current.

faq.question: Can it inspect email templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explains how including template data can provide email HTML or text content when supported by the tool response.

faq.question: What permissions are needed?
faq.answer: The skill notes that campaign read scope is required for the documented campaign workflows.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive campaign data?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill routes Klaviyo access through Rube MCP, so users should verify authorization and data handling before use.
